preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the bilabial nasal [m] is primarily documented as a form of nasal assimilation in consonant clusters, where the sibilant adopts the nasal and labial features of a following segment.

examples.note: Historical assimilation where the sibilant before a nasal became nasalized or completely merged into the nasal sound.
Occasional assimilation in rapid speech across word boundaries where the voiced fricative is absorbed into the following labial nasal.

sources: Phonological research on Greek and Arabic nasal-sibilant clusters.
